Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given an event log table event_log with columns case_id activity timestamp We need to 1 Identify cases that contain the direct succession sequence Approve Purchase Requisition Create Order Confirm Order with no other events in between 2 Among those keep only cases where the time between Create Order and Confirm Order is more than 5 days 3 Exclude those cases from the final result Return all events from cases that do NOT meet the above criteria

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Approach We can use window functions to detect the sequence Since we need direct succession we can use LAG and LEAD to check the previous and next activities within the same case ordered by timestamp

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Steps 1 For each event we can check if it is the middle event Create Order and its previous event is Approve Purchase Requisition and next event is Confirm Order 2 Then for such events compute the time difference between the Confirm Order next event and Create Order current event 3 Flag cases that have at least one such sequence with time difference more than 5 days 4 Finally select all events from cases that are not flagged

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: However note There might be multiple sequences in a case The problem says contains the following sequence in direct succession If a case has multiple such sequences we only need at least one that satisfies the time condition to exclude the case

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Implementation We can create a CTE that identifies the middle events of the sequence and computes the time difference Then we get the distinct case_ids that meet the condition Finally we select from event_log where case_id not in those case_ids

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Note DuckDB supports window functions and CTEs We will order by timestamp within each case_id Important The sequence must be in direct succession so we check LAG activity OVER PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY timestamp equals Approve Purchase Requisition activity equals Create Order LEAD activity OVER PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY timestamp equals Confirm Order Then for such rows we can get the timestamp of the next event LEAD timestamp and compute the difference
